calendar.matters:
Verified Petition To Recover Unpaid Rent And Penalty
Off Calendar Feb-11-2019 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Approving Settlement
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Catherine Meullemans for Karen Apana (Trustee).
Mark Watson appeared by telephone for Golf Club LLC.

Ms. Meullemans stated that the settlement has been signed by all parties and provided a proposed order. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Settlement (subject to review of the order). The Petition to Recover Unpaid Rent on calendar for February 20, 2018 is off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Verified Petition To Recover Unpaid Rent And Penalty
Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Continued To Feb-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Application For An Order Shortening Time For Hearing On Petition For Order Approving Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Denied

Verified Petition To Recover Unpaid Rent And Penalty
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Continued To Jan-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Watson for Gough Club, LLC

Mr. Watson stated that they settled the case on Thursday, that Mr. Bacon will be filing a petition for approval of the settlement, that they have an agreement to file said petition and that if Mr. Bacon does not file it, he will. The Petition to Recover Unpaid Rent, etc. is continued to January 7, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

Verified Petition To Recover Unpaid Rent And Penalty
Off Calendar Sep-10-2018 Continued To Dec-03-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roxana Jauregui appeared by telephone for Gough Club, LLC.
Theodore Bacon appeared by telephone for Karen Apana (Trustee).

Mr. Bacon requested a continuance stating that they have agreed to mediate, that they have mediation scheduled for November 1st and that they have a couple of other dates in mind in November. The Petition to Recover Unpaid Rent and Penalty is continued to December 3, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation and trial setting.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Verified Petition To Recover Unpaid Rent And Penalty
Off Calendar Mar-21-2018 Continued To Sep-10-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Catherine Meulemans for Karen Apana (Tustee)
Kimberly Sullivan for Gough Club, LLC (Petitioner)

Ms. Sullivan stated that some discovery went out, she received some responses, have depositions to be completed within 90 days, and requesedt trial date (1-2 days) for October. Ms. Meulemans stated that she would like to file a dispositive motion regarding the legal issue of whether the guarantee survives the death of Ms. Wanderer. The Verified Petition to Recover Unpaid Rent and Penalty is continued to September 10, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status conference.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Verified Petition To Recover Unpaid Rent And Penalty
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Continued To Mar-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kimberly Sullivan appeared by telephone for Gough Club, LLC (Petitioner).
Catherine Meulemans appeared by telephone for Karen Apana (Respondent/Trustee).

Ms. Sullivan stated that they have some written discovery going out and that they have to take one deposition. Ms. Meulemans stated that this is primarily a legal case, that she does not see a need for discovery and that she would like to file a motion for summary judgment. Ms. Sullivan stated that she needs 60-days to complete discovery and the month of June for trial. The Petition to Recover Unpaid Rent and Penalty is continued to March 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Verified Petition To Recover Unpaid Rent And Penalty
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Continued To Jan-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Watson for Petitioner.
Theodore Bacon appeared by telephone for Respondent.

The Court stated that this appears to be a breach of contract and that it should be filed in Civil Court. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the matter remaining in Probate Court making reference to Probate Code 850(a)(3)(C), Probate Code 17000(b) and Probate Code 19008. The Verified Petition to Recover Unpaid Rent and Penalty is taken under submission for the Court to determine the issue of whether or not the matter is to remain here or to be filed in Civil Court.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
